news.headline: Panama authorizes export of 120,000 tons of copper concentrate from Cobre Panama mine, seeking resolution and reimbursement from First Quantum Minerals.
news.summary: The President of Panama, Jose Raul Mulino, has allowed for the export of 120,000 tons of copper concentrate that have been stored at the Cobre Panama mine since its closure in December 2023. Mulino stated that this action was necessary as the material was being wasted and a significant amount of money had been invested in it by Panama. He emphasized the importance of handling the mine issue responsibly, considering national interests. The mine is operated by First Quantum Minerals, a Canadian company, which will be required to reimburse Panama for the material once it is processed abroad. Former President Laurentino Cortizo had initially shut down the Cobre Panama mine.

news.headline: Hudson Global reported 2024 results: Q4 revenue $33.6M, FY revenue $140.1M. Q4 net loss $0.6M, FY net loss $4.8M. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $0.9M in Q4 vs $0.1M in 2023. Q4 Asia Pacific revenue $20M, EMEA revenue $6.3M. Cash and cash equivalents $17.7M. Hudson invested $3.4M in sales, marketing, and technology in 2024. Hudson purchased 154,084 shares for $2.5M in 2024 under the share repurchase program. NOL Carryforward of $240M as of Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: Hudson Global, Inc. reported its financial results for Q4 and full year 2024. In Q4, revenue was $33.6 million (1.1% decrease from Q4 2023), with adjusted net revenue at $17.6 million (6.4% increase from Q4 2023). Net loss in Q4 was $0.6 million, an adjusted EBITDA of $0.9 million. For the full year, revenue was $140.1 million (13.2% decrease from 2023), adjusted net revenue was $70.2 million, net loss was $4.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $0.9 million. The company invested $3.4 million in sales, marketing, and technology. Americas showed growth, with revenue at $27.9 million, adjusted net revenue at $25.1 million, and EBITDA at $0.3 million for the full year 2024. Asia Pacific revenue was $86.7 million, adjusted net revenue was $29.4 million, and EBITDA was $0.5 million for 2024. EMEA revenue was $25.5 million, adjusted net revenue was $15.6 million, and EBITDA was $0.3 million for 2024. The Company had $17.7 million in cash at the end of Q4 2024 and had used $2.8 million in cash flow from operations for the full year 2024. They also repurchased $2.5 million worth of shares and had $2.1 million remaining under the $5 million share repurchase program. Hudson Global had $240 million in usable net operating losses.

news.headline: Senator Schumer did not have a plan for the budget fight, according to new reports from Bloomberg.
NoMIS Power in Albany, NY achieves breakthrough in improving short-circuit withstand time of SiC MOSFETs, a key challenge.
news.summary: Albany, New York, March 14, 2025 - NoMIS Power, a renowned company specializing in advanced silicon carbide (SiC) power semiconductor technology, recently revealed a significant advancement in enhancing the short-circuit withstand time (SCWT) of SiC MOSFETs. Sidus Space LizzieSat™-3 to launch March 14, 2025, as part of Transporter-13 rideshare mission with SpaceX from Vandenberg Space Force Base. Launch window opens March 14, 2024, at 11:39 PM PDT.
news.summary: Based in Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, Sidus Space, a prominent player in space missions (NASDAQ: SIDU), is all set to launch the LizzieSat™-3 satellite on March 14, 2024, beginning at 11:39 PM PDT. This satellite, part of the Transporter-13 rideshare mission with SpaceX, will take off from Space Launch Complex 4 East. Follow live mission updates on X (previously Twitter) @SpaceX.

news.headline: Global structural heart devices market valued at US$14.93B in 2023, set to grow at a CAGR of 9.5% to reach US$25.69B by 2029. Dominant segment is transcatheter heart valves; North America leads market share.
news.summary: The global structural heart devices market was valued at US$14.93 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 9.5%, reaching US$16.31 billion in 2024 and a remarkable US$25.69 billion by 2029. The growth is attributed to the rise in valvular and congenital heart diseases, advancements in minimally invasive technologies like TAVR and TMVR, and increasing adoption due to an aging population and improved healthcare infrastructure worldwide. The market is segmented into Heart Valve Devices, Occluder, Closure systems, Delivery systems, and Annuloplasty rings. The Transcatheter Heart Valve segment is particularly dominant due to factors like increasing prevalence of valvular heart diseases, aging population, technological innovations, and preference for minimally invasive procedures. North America leads in market share, driven by a sophisticated healthcare infrastructure. Key players in the market are mentioned in the Benzinga.com article.

news.summary: Initial findings from the five single ascending dose (SAD) groups and the initial three complete multiple ascending dose (MAD) groups indicate positive results, confirming the safety and tolerability of PALI-2108.

Li Auto reported fiscal Q4 2024 revenue of 44.3 billion CNY, missing estimates. Adjusted net earnings were 3.79 CNY/ADS. Vehicle sales rose to $5.8 billion, with 500K deliveries in 2024.
news.summary: Li Auto Inc, traded on NASDAQ as LI, released its fiscal fourth-quarter 2024 results, indicating a revenue growth of 6.1% year-on-year to 44.3 billion Chinese yuan ($6.07 billion), slightly below the analyst consensus of 44.6 billion Chinese yuan. Their adjusted net earnings per ADS stood at 3.79 Chinese yuan ($0.52), surpassing the analyst projection of 2.87 Chinese yuan. However, the stock price declined post-announcement. Vehicle sales also saw a 5.6% increase, reaching $5.8 billion, attributed to higher vehicle deliveries despite a reduction in the average selling price, primarily due to product variations.

news.headline: Global biodiesel market forecasted to reach $70.61 billion by 2033 from $41.62 billion in 2024, with 6.05% CAGR. Regulations, energy security driving growth.
news.summary: The report on the Biodiesel Market projects a growth trajectory, expecting it to reach a value of US$ 70.61 billion by the year 2033, rising from US$ 41.62 billion in 2024, showcasing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.05% from 2025 to 2033. Various factors are propelling this market expansion, such as governmental policies favoring renewable energy sources, escalating anxieties regarding energy stability, and the unpredictable nature of oil prices.

DOMA Perpetual nominates 3 candidates for Pacira's board. Stock down 76% in a decade, with $500M cost due to CEO's actions. CEO pushing for higher compensation.
news.summary: Over the past ten years, Pacira's stock has plunged by a staggering 76%. During this time, the board and management team have pocketed around $500 million, which accounts for approximately half of the current market capitalization. Frank Lee, who serves as both the CEO and a board member, has overseen a 33% drop in the stock price. Despite the company's dismal performance, Lee is advocating for increased compensation for himself and the executives.

Adjust releases 2025 Gaming App Insights report, showing 4% YoY increase in global gaming app installs in 2024.
news.summary: In San Francisco, a prominent measurement and analytics firm, Adjust, unveiled its Gaming App Insights 2025 report, demonstrating the robustness of the gaming sector post a challenging 2023. The global gaming app installations saw a 4% Year-on-Year rise in 2024, while sessions experienced a slight decrease of 0.6%. The year 2025 signifies an opportunity to sustain this growth trend by enhancing essential metrics such as retention rates and user engagement. Following a phase of instability in the market, mobile gaming appears to be resuming an upward trajectory.

news.headline: Californian gets sentence for laundering Dark Web MDMA profits with Bitcoin amid Operation Crypto Runner, a multi-year investigation in U.S.
news.summary: An individual was apprehended as part of Operation Crypto Runner, a collaborative effort between multiple agencies spanning several years focused on apprehending cryptocurrency money launderers within the United States.

news.headline: FTX liquidated $1.5B in 3AC assets before hedge fund collapsed, now seeking $1.53B claim, while creditors still owed $3.3B.
news.summary: New court documents have revealed that FTX liquidated $1.53 billion in Three Arrows Capital (3AC) assets just before the hedge fund's collapse in mid-2022, challenging the notion that 3AC's downfall was solely market-driven. Despite being valued at over $10 billion, 3AC collapsed after leveraged directional trades went wrong, and it had borrowed from 20 institutions prior to the May 2022 crypto crash. 3AC recently sought to increase its claim against FTX from $120 million to $1.53 billion after discovering the undisclosed liquidation. A British Virgin Islands court froze $1.14 billion worth of 3AC co-founders assets, with Teneo estimating creditors are still owed roughly $3.3 billion. Financial analysts believe even with the additional $1.5 billion, 3AC may not have been able to meet creditor claims. Although 3AC can pursue the larger claim, it is uncertain how much they will ultimately receive. Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao called the news an "interesting turn of events." The collapse of 3AC followed Terraform Labs' collapse and preceded Celsius halting user withdrawals.

news.headline: Rubrik stock surges on Q4 results with narrower loss and exceeded revenue, defying software industry trend.
news.summary: Rubrik's stock saw a surge following the data security software company's announcement of a Q4 loss that was smaller than anticipated and revenue exceeding expectations. The positive financial report led to the stock's rise, indicating strong investor confidence in the company's performance. Additionally, Rubrik's optimistic fiscal 2026 outlook contributed to the stock's upward momentum, contrasting the overall downtrend observed in software stocks during the period.

news.headline: Pfizer expects first-quarter sales and earnings to slide. Question arises if PFE stock is now a sell.
news.summary: Given the projected decrease in first-quarter sales and earnings, is it recommended to sell Pfizer stock at this time? PFE stock may be viewed as a sell due to the anticipated slowdown in first-quarter performance.

news.headline: EV sales challenging oil demand in Trump's 'Drill, Baby, Drill' era. Are they affecting prices?
news.summary: Are electric vehicles (EVs) starting to impact oil prices and demand, as they were expected to do? Recent data suggests that the rise in EV sales is beginning to raise doubts about the future demand for oil in a world that previously embraced the "Drill, Baby, Drill" mentality. This shift in consumer behavior is now raising questions about the long-term viability of oil stocks, as EVs continue to gain ground in the automotive market. As the trend towards cleaner energy sources accelerates, oil companies may need to reassess their strategies to adapt to this changing landscape.

news.headline: Dow Jones rose on Schumer's comments; Nvidia, Tesla rallied. Positive sentiment led to gains on Friday.
news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones increased following Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer's decision to withdraw his threat of blocking a GOP spending bill. This move led to a rally in Nvidia and Tesla stocks. The Dow Jones Futures saw a rise in response to Schumer's comments, indicating positive market reactions.

news.headline: USA-UK alliance introduces MARS™ AI model by Enter Growl LLC for mission-critical applications, countering China.
news.summary: Growl LLC introduced their new Multi-Agent Reasoning Service, called MARS™, aiming to enhance reliability and explainability for crucial AI applications. MARS™ is a governance AI model which is future-proof and excels at aligning various agents to improve overall performance. This launch marks a significant step forward in advancing AI capabilities in mission-critical scenarios.

news.headline: Biolexis Therapeutics discovers new GLP-1 activation mode using CryoEM, validating MolecuLern™ AI, a key advancement in drug discovery.
news.summary: Biolexis Therapeutics, based in LEHI, Utah, made a significant breakthrough in metabolic drug discovery on March 14, 2025. Through the use of CryoEM technology, they confirmed the first-in-class allosteric binding mode, validating the effectiveness of their MolecuLern™ AI-driven discovery process. This achievement marks a key advancement in the field, showcasing the power of innovative approaches in drug development.

Trade disputes increase between Europe and US as tariffs rise, with $7.5 billion in tariffs imposed on EU goods.
Water for Injection market to hit US$ 71.7B by 2035, increasing from US$ 30.5B in 2024, at 8.0% CAGR. Need rising in healthcare due to stringent requirements, biopharmaceutical production.
news.summary: The Water for Injection market is experiencing significant growth driven by the demand for high-purity water in pharmaceutical manufacturing and biotechnology sectors. In 2024, the market was valued at US$ 30.5 billion and is expected to grow at an 8.0% CAGR to reach US$ 71.7 billion by 2035. Factors fueling this growth include stringent regulatory standards, increasing biopharmaceutical production, and advancements in sterile water purification techniques like distillation and membrane-based reverse osmosis. The market players are focusing on technological innovation and strategic partnerships to meet the escalating demand for injectables. Key industry participants include Eurocrit Labs International, Veolia Water Solutions and Technologies, and Thermo Fisher Scientific, Inc. Major market drivers include the booming biopharmaceutical and biosimilar markets, as well as the adoption of single-use systems in pharmaceutical manufacturing. Notable recent developments include Veolia's Polaris 2.0 systems and WuXi STA's expansion of its sterile manufacturing line in Asia.

news.headline: JetZero appoints Julie Felgar as Head of Government Affairs to oversee government relations and community affairs in aviation and aerospace.
news.summary: In Long Beach, California, on March 14, 2025, JetZero announced the appointment of Julie Felgar as the new Head of Government Affairs. In her role, she will be responsible for managing federal, state, and local government relationships as well as community affairs. With extensive knowledge in aviation and aerospace policy, Ms. Felgar is well-equipped to handle the various aspects of her new position at JetZero.
